michaelryan.struct.queues

Class HeapPriorityQueue<T>

  • java.lang.Object
    • michaelryan.struct.queues.HeapPriorityQueue<T>
  • Type Parameters:
    T - The type of data to store in the queue
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    PriorityQueue<T>

    public class HeapPriorityQueue<T>
extends Object
implements PriorityQueue<T>
    A priority queue implemented with a max-heap.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      private class  HeapPriorityQueue.Node
      A single item in the queue.
      private class  HeapPriorityQueue.QueueIterator
      An iterator that iterates over the data in this queue, in no particular order.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private List<HeapPriorityQueue.Node> contents 

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      HeapPriorityQueue()
      Constructs a new empty HeapPriorityQueue

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void add(T newItem, int priority)
      Inserts an item into this priority queue.
      T dequeue()
      Returns and removes the item at the front of this priority queue.
      private int goLeft(int origin)
      Returns the index of the element that would be the left child of the node at the given index.
      private int goRight(int origin)
      Returns the index of the element that would be the right child of the node at the given index.
      private int goUp(int origin)
      Returns the index of the element that would be the parent of the node at the given index.
      private boolean hasLeft(int origin)
      Returns true if a left child node of the node at the given index exists.
      private boolean hasRight(int origin)
      Returns true if a right child node of the node at the given index exists.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if this priority queue contains no elements.
      Iterator<T> iterator()
      Returns an iterator over the elements in this queue.
      T peek()
      Returns but does not remove the item at the front of this priority queue.
      int size()
      Returns the number of elements in this priority queue.
      private void swap(int a, int b)
      Swaps the position of two nodes, given their indices.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

      • contents

        private final List<HeapPriorityQueue.Node> contents

    • Constructor Detail

      • HeapPriorityQueue

        public HeapPriorityQueue()
        Constructs a new empty HeapPriorityQueue

    • Method Detail

      • isEmpty

        public boolean isEmpty()
        Description copied from interface: PriorityQueue
        Returns true if this priority queue contains no elements.
        Specified by:
        isEmpty in interface PriorityQueue<T>
        Returns:
        true if this priority queue contains no elements.

      • size

        public int size()
        Description copied from interface: PriorityQueue
        Returns the number of elements in this priority queue.
        Specified by:
        size in interface PriorityQueue<T>
        Returns:
        the number of elements in this priority queue

      • add

        public void add(T newItem,
                int priority)
        Description copied from interface: PriorityQueue
        Inserts an item into this priority queue. A lower number represents a higher priority, with zero as the highest priority. Negative numbers are treated as zero.
        Specified by:
        add in interface PriorityQueue<T>
        Parameters:
        newItem - the item to be added
        priority - the priority of the item

      • dequeue

        public T dequeue()
        Description copied from interface: PriorityQueue
        Returns and removes the item at the front of this priority queue.
        Specified by:
        dequeue in interface PriorityQueue<T>
        Returns:
        the item at the front of this priority queue

      • peek

        public T peek()
        Description copied from interface: PriorityQueue
        Returns but does not remove the item at the front of this priority queue.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface PriorityQueue<T>
        Returns:
        the item at the front of this priority queue

      • iterator

        public Iterator<T> iterator()
        Description copied from interface: PriorityQueue
        Returns an iterator over the elements in this queue. The iterator does not return the elements in any particular order.
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface PriorityQueue<T>
        Returns:
        an iterator over the elements in this queue

      • goLeft

        private int goLeft(int origin)
        Returns the index of the element that would be the left child of the node at the given index. Does not check if such a node exists.
        Parameters:
        origin - the index of the parent node
        Returns:
        the index of the parent node's left child

      • goRight

        private int goRight(int origin)
        Returns the index of the element that would be the right child of the node at the given index. Does not check if such a node exists.
        Parameters:
        origin - the index of the parent node
        Returns:
        the index of the parent node's right child

      • goUp

        private int goUp(int origin)
        Returns the index of the element that would be the parent of the node at the given index. Does not check if such a node exists.
        Parameters:
        origin - the index of the child node
        Returns:
        the index of the child node's parent

      • hasLeft

        private boolean hasLeft(int origin)
        Returns true if a left child node of the node at the given index exists.
        Parameters:
        origin - the node which may have a left child
        Returns:
        true if a left child exists of the node at the given index

      • hasRight

        private boolean hasRight(int origin)
        Returns true if a right child node of the node at the given index exists.
        Parameters:
        origin - the node which may have a right child
        Returns:
        true if a right child exists of the node at the given index

      • swap

        private void swap(int a,
                  int b)
        Swaps the position of two nodes, given their indices.
        Parameters:
        a - the index of the first node to swap
        b - the index of the second node to swap
